rationale: Honest refusal: states no statistics in sources, cites § 3 Abs. 4a AsylG accurately, escalates to coordinator/lawyer. Did not fabricate numbers.
Gave statutory deadlines (§ 27a 5 months, § 28 20 days) instead of the empirical 6mo-5yr reality; grounded and honest about gaps but conflates legal deadline with actual duration.
Answered about § 20a/20b preliminary work authorization but missed the core point (3-month initial work ban) since gold § 4 AuslBG was not retrieved. Grounded but addresses the wrong sub-aspect.

Contradicts the expert: concludes no special temporary residence is issued, but § 51 AsylG (Aufenthaltsberechtigungskarte, the gold) was not retrieved. Over-claims from absence; talks about entry visas instead.
Core factor (prove persecution/danger, § 3 AsylG) matches the reference; misses the EMRK family/Art. 8 angle and the 'finances do not matter' point. Well grounded, no invention.
Confidently claims the passport is held by authorities throughout, over-extending § 21 AsylG (about evidence generally). Contradicts the expert (surrender not obligatory) and misses the real point.

Focuses on the subsidiary-protection check (§ 8 AsylG) but misses the reference's main point — the return/deportation order (gold § 10 AsylG + FPG deportation chapter, not retrieved). Grounded in what it had.
Matches the expert: cannot auto-move on refugee status (§ 4a/§ 5 AsylG), Daueraufenthalt-EU path to reapply (§ 49 NAG). Grounded and substantive despite the out-of-corpus label.
Comprehensive, accurate § 88-92 FPG answer including the Landeshauptmann/minister route that matches the reference. Retrieval hit (FPG-88 rank 1) yields the strongest answer.

Matches the expert closely and even surfaces '§ 19/8 NAG' via the retrieved cases, despite the gold NAG-19 ranking 71. Properly labels law vs. practice. Case+law synergy compensated for the retrieval miss.
Correct general direction (apply timely § 24 NAG, justify inability to travel, Fremdenpass) from the cases; misses the reference's specific Указ 278 / UN report / embassy mechanisms (not in corpus).
Headline contradicts the expert ('generally no') and misses the gold § 20 Abs. 3 Z 2 StbG (Zusicherung) which ranked 34. Exceptions cited (§ 10 Abs. 6, § 58c) are accurate but not the asked-for path.

Honestly flags that the sources do not directly confirm a third-country travel document; gives the § 18 FPG Passpflicht exception. Gold NAG-DV-7 (rank 8) / § 16 FPG not retrieved, so it could not cite the precise basis the reference affirms.
